description_long: This is a roughly conical, wide-mouthed bowl with an irregular, jagged rim and a visibly weathered surface. The bowl appears to be made from a dark, coarse ceramic or stone material, with several visible cracks and chips along its edges and body. The surface is matte and uneven, with a natural, earthy coloration and no visible glaze. There are two white labels affixed to the inner and outer surfaces, featuring handwritten text, likely for cataloging or identification purposes. The interior is deep and tapers toward a narrower, uneven base, suggesting it may have been hand-formed or reconstructed from fragments. The overall appearance is utilitarian and aged, with clear signs of wear and damage.
description: A wide-mouthed, irregularly shaped bowl made of dark, coarse ceramic or stone, with a jagged rim and visible cracks. The surface is matte and weathered, featuring catalog labels and signs of age.
description_view_0: This view highlights the bowl's wide opening, jagged rim, and rough, weathered surface, with the interior appearing deep and shadowed.
description_view_1: This angle shows the bowl's uneven rim and a prominent white label on the inner surface, as well as visible cracks and chips along the body.
description_view_2: From above, the bowl's irregular shape and two white labels are clearly visible, emphasizing the asymmetry and damaged areas.
description_view_3: This view displays the bowl's rough exterior, the placement of the labels, and the pronounced cracks and chips on the rim and body.
attribute_location: archaeological site, museum storage, or outdoor urban excavation area
attribute_context_of_use: holding or displaying small items, possibly for archaeological or historical study

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
